message:
fix(decoding): retain cumulative symbol count across block completions

DecodingPipeline::progress().symbols_received and the InsufficientSymbols
error message previously reported self.symbols.len(), which returns the
count of symbols currently held in the SymbolSet. When a block completes,
its symbols are consumed and removed from the set, causing the reported
count to drop even though more symbols have been received overall. This
made progress tracking non-monotonic and produced confusing error messages
like "received 4, needed 8" when the pipeline had actually received 4
symbols in block 0 (consumed) plus 0 symbols in block 1.

A new accepted_symbols_total counter tracks the cumulative number of
symbols accepted by feed(), independent of whether they have been
consumed by block decoding. Both progress() and into_data() now report
this counter.

Test added: multi_block_progress_retains_cumulative_symbols_after_block_completion
encodes a 2-block object, feeds all block-0 symbols, verifies
symbols_received stays at 4 after block 0 completes, and confirms that
into_data() correctly reports InsufficientSymbols { received: 4, needed: 8 }.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
